questions.question_text: Gender:
Age: (age at the time of inpatient episode)
What was the route of patient contact?

answer_options: Obstetrics and Gynaecology (but not maternity services)
questions.question_text: What specialty was the patient's care under? Please select the specialty they are currently under, or if discharged, the specialty they were under at discharge.

Is smoking status documented in the patient's notes? 'Smoking' refers to conventional smoking – cigarettes, cigars, pipes etc. 'Notes' includes medical, nursing or allied health professional (AHP) notes for the current admission, including any preadmission assessment clerking proformas relating to the current admission.

If the answer to 5a is yes, is the patient a current smoker? 'Smoking' refers to conventional smoking – cigarettes, cigars, pipes etc. 'Current smoker' refers to any patient currently smoking or smoked within the last 4 weeks – please answer questions 7-10 for these patients.

Is vaping status documented in the patient's notes? 'Vaping' refers to the use of electronic cigarettes. 'Notes' includes medical, nursing or allied health professional (AHP) notes for the current admission, including any preadmission assessment clerking proformas relating to the current admission.
If the answer to 5c is yes, is the patient a current vaper? 'Current vaper' refers to any patient currently vaping or vaped within the last 4 weeks. Please answer question 13 for these patients.
Is the patient's non-cigarette smoking status documented in the notes? (for example, shisha, marijuana, does not include vaping)

If the answer to 6a is yes, what forms of non-cigarette smoking have been documented in the notes? Tick all that apply 'Heat not burn products' are an electrically-heated smoking system that releases smoke by heating tobacco at a lower temperature than a conventional cigarette.
Other non-cigarette smoking forms - please specify:

answer_options: Yes, they were provided very brief advice
questions.question_text: After identifying the patient is a current smoker, is there evidence in the patient's notes that they were provided with 'Very Brief Advice'?
Where was the patient referred?
Other referral location - please specify:
If the patient was referred to a hospital smoking cessation service – were they reviewed by that service during the admission?
Is there evidence in the patient's notes or drug chart that they were offered the use of licensed pharmacotherapy for tobacco addiction to help them abstain whilst an inpatient?

answer_options: Single agent NRT (short acting or long acting patch)
Combination NRT (short acting and long acting patch)
questions.question_text: If the answer to 9a is yes, what pharmacotherapy for tobacco addiction was provided whilst an inpatient? Tick all that apply 'NRT' = Nicotine replacement therapy, for example skin patches, chewing gum, lozenge, oral strips, nasal / mouth spray

answer_options: Evidence ion the notes that the option of vaping discussed with the patient

Patient declined the offer of a vaping kit as a smoking cessation tool

If the answer to 10a is yes, what pharmacotherapy for tobacco addiction was the patient discharged with? Tick all that apply
Did the patient attend any smoking cessation services for follow-up after discharge?
Was the patient abstinent at 4 weeks after discharge?

answer_options: Patient allowed to use existing vape kit during admission
